policy: Use volume as a broad guardrail after stage and family selection, never as the first decision.
relative_buoyancy_notes: More litres generally add buoyancy and paddle support, but where the foam sits changes stability and response.
A short wide board can carry many litres while remaining unsuitable for a true beginner.
A longer board can provide better glide and learning value without an extreme litre number.
Fitness, age, wetsuit, wave power and frequency influence practical needs.

STAGE_1_TRUE_BEGINNER.warning: Do not reuse an advanced saved-volume reference.

STAGE_5_ADVANCED.precision: fine-grained model and condition tuning permitted

unsafe_patterns: weight alone -> exact litres -> closest stocked board
closest volume -> Strong match without family safety
